rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ERNEST V RHINE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To First Amended Will Contest And Grounds Of Objection To Probate Of Will Of Ernest V. Rhine On The Ground Of Undue Influence
rulings.Rulings: Respondents' Demurrer to Lydia Marquez Rhine's First Amended Will Contest and Grounds of Objection to Probate of Will of Ernest V. Rhine on the Ground of Undue Influence is OVERRULED for the following reasons: (1) Lydia has standing under Probate Code §48(a) as she has pecuniary interest as a surviving spouse in a will contest, and also as a potentially pretermitted spouse with respect to a petition for distribution in the event that the will contest is unsuccessful. (Katleman v. Crowley (1993) 13 Cal.App.4th 51.) (2) Lydia has sufficiently alleged all the elements of a cause of action for undue influence. The allegations concerning undue influence are clear enough that Respondents attempted to counter those allegations in their moving papers, which demonstrates, among other things, that the undue influence claim was sufficiently pleaded.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELINORE GREEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, despite Petitioner's argument that the decedent's pre-deceased husband did not need to be listed in item 8 of the petition, where the Judicial Council form clearly indicates that all persons mentioned in the will, whether living or deceased, must be listed, and LRSF 14.26 E also requires that any predeceased devisee must be listed with his or her date of death. Status of closing estate set for 11/6/19.
